---
title: "OJS-test"
author: "Andy Grogan-Kaylor"
date: "today"
format: html
---

```{r}
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true

library(readr) # read CSV

library(tidyr) # tidy data

library(dplyr) # data wrangling, and for pipe %>%

patterns <- read_csv("patterns.csv")

patterns <- patterns %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number())

patterns$Item <- factor(patterns$Item)

patterns$Item <- factor(patterns$Item, 
                        levels = c("Shouted at child", 
                                   "Called child names", 
                                   "Shook child", 
                                   "Spanked child",
                                   "Hit/slapped child on arm/leg",
                                   "Hit child with object",
                                   "Hit/slapped child on face/head/ears",
                                   "Beat as hard as one could",
                                   "Took privileges away from child",
                                   "Explained why behavior was wrong",
                                   "Gave child something else to do"))

patterns2 <- patterns %>% 
  pivot_longer(!c(Item, row_id), 
               names_to = "discipline", 
               values_to = "value")

write_csv(patterns2, "patterns2.csv")

patterns2L <- patterns2 %>% 
  filter(discipline == "Class 3\nLower behavioral control")

write_csv(patterns2L, "patterns2L.csv")

patterns2H <- patterns2 %>% 
  filter(discipline == "Class 1\nHigh behavioral control")

write_csv(patterns2H, "patterns2H.csv")

```


```{ojs}
//| code-fold: true

data = FileAttachment("patterns2.csv").csv({ typed: true })

dataL = FileAttachment("patterns2L.csv").csv({ typed: true })

dataH = FileAttachment("patterns2H.csv").csv({ typed: true })

```

```{ojs}
//| code-fold: true
//| fig-cap: "Patterns of Parental Discipline"

Plot.plot({
  x: {axis: null, domain: ["Shouted at child", "Called child names", "Shook child", "Spanked child", "Hit/slapped child on arm/leg", "Hit child with object", "Hit/slapped child on face/head/ears", "Beat as hard as one could", "Took privileges away from child", "Explained why behavior was wrong", "Gave child something else to do"]},
  y: {tickFormat: "p", grid: true},
  fx: {label: null},
  color: {
    domain: ["Shouted at child", "Called child names", "Shook child", "Spanked child", "Hit/slapped child on arm/leg", "Hit child with object", "Hit/slapped child on face/head/ears", "Beat as hard as one could", "Took privileges away from child", "Explained why behavior was wrong", "Gave child something else to do"],
    range: [ "#4147ADFF", "#4777EFFF", "#39A4FBFF", "#FE9B2DFF", "#F46516FF", "#DB3A07FF", "#B21A01FF", "#7A0403FF", "#62FC6BFF", "#A2FC3CFF", "#D2E935FF"], // Your custom hex codes
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(data, {
      x: "Item",
      y: "value",
      fill: "Item",
      fx: "discipline",
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})

```

```{ojs}
//| code-fold: true
//| fig-cap: "Lower Behavioral Control"

Plot.plot({
  x: {axis: null, domain: ["Shouted at child", "Called child names", "Shook child", "Spanked child", "Hit/slapped child on arm/leg", "Hit child with object", "Hit/slapped child on face/head/ears", "Beat as hard as one could", "Took privileges away from child", "Explained why behavior was wrong", "Gave child something else to do"]},
  y: {tickFormat: "p", grid: true},
  fx: {label: null},
  color: {
    domain: ["Shouted at child", "Called child names", "Shook child", "Spanked child", "Hit/slapped child on arm/leg", "Hit child with object", "Hit/slapped child on face/head/ears", "Beat as hard as one could", "Took privileges away from child", "Explained why behavior was wrong", "Gave child something else to do"],
    range: [ "#4147ADFF", "#4777EFFF", "#39A4FBFF", "#FE9B2DFF", "#F46516FF", "#DB3A07FF", "#B21A01FF", "#7A0403FF", "#62FC6BFF", "#A2FC3CFF", "#D2E935FF"], // Your custom hex codes
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(dataL, {
      x: "Item",
      y: "value",
      fill: "Item",
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})

```

```{ojs}
//| code-fold: true
//| fig-cap: "High Behavioral Control"

Plot.plot({
  x: {axis: null, domain: ["Shouted at child", "Called child names", "Shook child", "Spanked child", "Hit/slapped child on arm/leg", "Hit child with object", "Hit/slapped child on face/head/ears", "Beat as hard as one could", "Took privileges away from child", "Explained why behavior was wrong", "Gave child something else to do"]},
  y: {tickFormat: "p", grid: true},
  fx: {label: null},
  color: {
    domain: ["Shouted at child", "Called child names", "Shook child", "Spanked child", "Hit/slapped child on arm/leg", "Hit child with object", "Hit/slapped child on face/head/ears", "Beat as hard as one could", "Took privileges away from child", "Explained why behavior was wrong", "Gave child something else to do"],
    range: [ "#4147ADFF", "#4777EFFF", "#39A4FBFF", "#FE9B2DFF", "#F46516FF", "#DB3A07FF", "#B21A01FF", "#7A0403FF", "#62FC6BFF", "#A2FC3CFF", "#D2E935FF"], // Your custom hex codes
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(dataH, {
      x: "Item",
      y: "value",
      fill: "Item",
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})

```
